config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan orang per orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang adil dan proporsional, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar seenaknya. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan, yang lemah biasanya paling dulu dirugikan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I believe individual rights should generally come first, because society can only be healthy if it protects the dignity and freedom of each person. From an American perspective, I am very wary of any argument that lets "the greater good" become a blank check for government or majorities to trample dissent, privacy, or minority rights. That said, rights are not absolute in every case, and public safety or emergencies can justify limited restrictions if they are narrow, lawful, and accountable. But as a rule, putting society above the individual is too easy to abuse, and history shows that the people with the least power pay the price first.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, sementara yang paling terdampak justru warga biasa dan kelompok kecil. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, proses yang adil, dan akuntabilitas yang jelas. Kalau tidak, “masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alasan untuk menekan orang yang paling lemah.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the idea that society should generally take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that framing makes me immediately suspicious because it has been used too often to justify overreach by governments and majorities, especially against dissenters and minority groups. I do think there are narrow situations like public health, emergencies, or immediate safety where limited restrictions are justified, but only if they are proportional, temporary, and accountable. If "society" becomes a catch-all excuse, then the people with the least power are usually the first ones hurt.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus berada di atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dalam konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk melegitimasi pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang paling sering menanggung akibatnya justru warga kecil dan kelompok rentan. Bagi saya, kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi harus tunduk pada hak dasar, proses yang adil, dan pengawasan yang jelas agar tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan. Pengecualian hanya bisa diterima kalau benar-benar sempit, sementara, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From an American perspective, I am very uneasy with any broad claim that the collective gets to override personal liberty, because that kind of logic has often been used to excuse censorship, discrimination, and government overreach. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are unlimited; in real emergencies like public health crises or imminent danger, narrowly tailored restrictions can be justified if they are temporary, transparent, and subject to checks. But if we let “the interests of society” become a default trump card, then the people with the least power are usually the first ones to lose.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
